title: Implement baseline and backtrack evidence

sources.reason: Defines baseline and backtrack evidence acceptance.
Existing deletion and protected-evidence backtrack guard.
Existing baseline commit and changed-path helpers.

riskAssessment.reason: Baseline evidence must distinguish unavailable historical data from measured regression without inventing values.

executionDecision.reason: User explicitly authorized the sequential Trust Layer execution plan.

preReviewWarnings: Missing baselines must be reported as unavailable; regressions must remain visible.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: Baseline evidence captures commit and capture time with test, coverage, scenario, file-count, and protected-asset fields.
Absent historical baselines are reported as unavailable without invented values.
Comparisons expose file deletion and quality regression before completion.
Baseline fixture, regression, missing-baseline, and evidence-chain tests pass.
guidelines: Baseline evidence must preserve unavailable values rather than inventing measurements.
Do not import internal modules from other features.

intent.problem: The current backtrack guard detects deletions but does not capture a structured pre-change baseline or compare quality and protected-asset evidence with commit and capture-time provenance.
constraints: Surface deletion and quality regression before finish.
intent.rationale: A provenance-bound baseline makes regression and missing history explicit instead of allowing silent quality drift.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User explicitly authorized the sequential Trust Layer plan.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
